page_description: Pick your path: full packages, day excursions, mainland safaris, or an AI trip planner. Browse Zanzibar and Tanzania destinations on the map and see how they fit together.

hero.lead: Use this page as the map: packages if you want everything arranged, excursions if you're already on Zanzibar, safaris if wildlife is the goal, and the planner if you're not sure yet.

local_guide.title: Where we send friends to eat.
local_guide.lead: From Stone Town to the north and south coasts, this is our current shortlist for the moments when a guest asks: where should we really eat — and what's worth planning around?
local_guide.coverage: {{areas}} areas · {{places}} hand-picked places · {{events}} dates to know
local_guide.note: Chosen the way our destination guides recommend a place to a friend: by mood, occasion and who you are travelling with.
local_guide.footer_title: Want us to narrow it down?
local_guide.footer_text: Tell us where you are staying, who you are with and what you feel like eating. We will suggest the best fit.

events.label: What's on
events.title: When the island celebrates.
events.lead: A few dates worth planning a trip around — from the nightly market to the island's big festivals.

items.name: Forodhani Night Market
items.description: Stone Town's seafront gardens turn into an open-air food market after sunset. Come hungry, browse the grills and eat standing up with the locals.

items.name: Sauti za Busara
items.description: East Africa's warmest music festival — pan-African line-ups inside the Old Fort and a carnival mood in the streets. Book Stone Town beds early.

items.name: Zanzibar International Film Festival
items.description: Open-air screenings, exhibitions and music take over the old town for a week. A lovely excuse to slow down in Stone Town for a night or two.

items.name: Mwaka Kogwa
items.description: The Shirazi new year: four days of rituals, mock fights and song in the island's south. Go with a local guide and ask before photographing.

items.name: Beach & Water Sports Festival
items.description: Dhow races, kite contests and beach games move along the coast for a week. Easy to combine with a beach stay in Paje or Nungwi.

items.name: Kendwa Full Moon Party
items.description: The island's best-known beach party, on the Saturday closest to the full moon. Stay in Kendwa or Nungwi so the night ends with a short walk home.

places.name: Lukmaan Restaurant
places.description: A lively, no-fuss stop for Zanzibari favourites. Point to what looks good, try a few dishes and come hungry. Best when atmosphere matters more than ceremony.

places.name: Taarab Restaurant
places.description: Choose this when you want dinner above the old town, with a little more occasion around it. A good fit for couples or an unhurried family evening.

places.name: Breadfruit Restaurant & Café
places.description: A comfortable central choice for a calmer meal after exploring the alleys. Useful when you want local ingredients in a more polished setting.

places.name: The Beach House Restaurant
places.description: Go for sea views, drinks and an easy sunset dinner on the edge of Stone Town. Reserve ahead if the timing of the sunset matters to you.

places.name: Fish Market Local Restaurant
places.description: A straightforward choice for seafood and local flavours without the resort formality. Come when you want the meal to feel relaxed and rooted in Nungwi.

places.name: Badolina Secret Garden
places.description: A sociable, easy-going spot that works well when your group wants variety. The leafy setting makes a welcome change from another table directly on the beach.

places.name: Terrace Nungwi
places.description: One for a longer evening with the water close by and a more considered menu. We would choose it when the setting is part of the celebration.

places.name: Mocco Beach Villa & Restaurant
places.description: A relaxed Kendwa option for lunch or dinner close to the sand. Useful when you want to stay in the rhythm of a beach day instead of dressing up for dinner.

places.name: Bin Jabir Restaurant
places.description: A warm, uncomplicated stop for Swahili food and seafood. A good choice when you want local cooking away from the hotel dining room.

places.name: Marlin Restaurant
places.description: Come for a relaxed meal centred on fish and seafood. It suits an easy lunch or dinner when the plan is simply to eat well and slow down.

places.name: Zanzibar Sunrise at Bandas
places.description: Choose it for a beach setting and an evening that can stretch a little longer. It is more about settling into the coast than rushing through courses.

places.name: La Base JungleKite
places.description: A laid-back option with an active, social feel. Good for a casual meal when you want something easy after time on the water.

places.name: The Island – Pongwe Restaurant
places.description: A tiny offshore setting that turns lunch into an outing of its own. Pick this for the experience and check access and timing before you set off.

places.name: The Cliff Grill
places.description: A good fit when you want a calm evening, sea air and a little more polish. Best for couples or families happy to take their time.

places.name: The Club by Pongwe Bay Resort
places.description: A dependable choice for an easy, comfortable meal in Pongwe. Useful when different ages and appetites need to work around one table.

places.name: Pongwe Beach Hotel Restaurant
places.description: Come for an unhurried meal beside one of the quieter bays on the east coast. It pairs naturally with a slow beach day.

places.name: Kae Funk Sunset Beach Bar
places.description: Go for music, sand and the west-facing sunset rather than a formal dinner. Arrive with time to settle in before the sky changes.

places.name: The Rock Restaurant Zanzibar
places.description: The setting is the reason to go: a restaurant on a rock just offshore at Pingwe. Book ahead and treat it as a Zanzibar experience, not only a meal.

places.name: Dreamland Restaurant & Bar
places.description: A relaxed alternative when you want friendly service and a straightforward dinner without turning the evening into a big event.

places.name: Sagando Restaurant & Bar
places.description: A comfortable local option for an easy evening in Michamvi. Good when the group wants familiar choices alongside island flavours.

places.name: Fadhil Restaurant
places.description: Come for straightforward Swahili cooking, grilled fish and curries. A good choice when you want something local, warm and unpretentious.

places.name: Lost Soles
places.description: A relaxed option for grilled tuna, seafood and familiar comfort food — especially useful when everyone around the table wants something different.

places.name: Ocean Boho House
places.description: Choose this for creative plates and a more polished dinner without losing the easy island mood. It is one we would reserve ahead.

places.name: Sharazad The View
places.description: A beautiful setting for a slow family lunch or sunset dinner. Come for the view as much as the food, and book ahead for the best timing.

places.name: Mr Kahawa
places.description: Our easy choice for coffee, breakfast or a relaxed lunch with the ocean in front of you. Go earlier if you prefer a quieter table.

places.name: Mapacha Food Court
places.description: A good answer for families and groups who cannot agree on one cuisine. The atmosphere is informal and everyone can choose their own direction.

places.name: Shanga Restaurant Paje
places.description: One to consider for local flavours and seafood in the centre of Paje, especially when you want dinner to feel rooted in the island.

places.name: Sahari Zanzibar
places.description: A quieter alternative to Paje for a beachside meal with a little more breathing room. A nice fit for couples or a slow family lunch.

places.name: Restaurant Stone Village & The Dream Garden
places.description: A welcoming choice for a relaxed dinner in Kizimkazi. Pick it when you want the evening to feel personal and away from the larger resorts.

places.name: Kameleon Blue Bar & Restaurant
places.description: A casual place for a slow lunch or uncomplicated dinner. It works well when the plan is to stay relaxed after a day on the south coast.

places.name: Karamba Restaurant
places.description: Come for the coastal setting and give yourself time. This is a good match for a long lunch rather than a quick stop between activities.

places.name: Fahari Off The Grid
places.description: A characterful option when you want to step away from the usual beach-hotel routine. Check the day's opening details before making the journey.

items.title: Packages
items.text: Best when you want one quote and the whole trip joined up. From {{price}} pp.

items.title: Excursions
items.text: Best when your hotel is booked and you need day trips. From {{price}} pp.

items.title: Safaris
items.text: Best when mainland wildlife is the anchor. Core routes from {{price}} pp.

paths.title: What are you trying to explore?
paths.lead: Most guests do not start with place names. Pick the kind of decision you need to make first.

items.title: I want the full trip arranged
items.text: Hotels, safaris, excursions, transfers, and special moments packaged into one plan.

items.title: I am already on the island
items.text: Day trips, dhow sails, Stone Town, spice farms, Jozani, snorkeling, culture, and ocean days.

items.title: I want mainland wildlife
items.text: Northern icons, southern wilderness, western chimp routes, quick fly-ins, Kilimanjaro, and cultural stops.

items.title: I need help choosing
items.text: Tell the AI planner your dates, pace, budget, and travel style. We turn the draft into a quote.

cta.title: Want us to map the right route?
cta.text: Tell us the places you like, your travel dates, and your pace. We'll connect the best packages, excursions, and safaris into one clear plan.

stone-town.title: Stone Town
stone-town.text: Best for guests who want history, food, carved doors, markets, rooftops, spice routes, and an easy first day on Zanzibar.

fumba.title: Fumba
fumba.text: The classic launch point for dhow sailing, sandbanks, snorkeling, seafood lunches, and slow Menai Bay days.

kizimkazi.title: Kizimkazi
kizimkazi.text: Best for early-morning dolphin trips and a quieter south-coast rhythm, often paired with Jozani or a beach lunch.

jozani.title: Jozani Forest
jozani.text: A gentle nature stop for red colobus monkeys, mangrove boardwalks, and forest time between beach or culture days.

nungwi.title: Nungwi
nungwi.text: The easiest north-coast base for wide beaches, sunsets, turtle stops, dhow yards, and Mnemba snorkeling days.

excursions.label: Mnemba Snorkeling & Trip to the North

matemwe.title: Matemwe
matemwe.text: A quieter northeast-coast base for reef days, Mnemba snorkeling, slower boutique stays, and ocean time without the busiest beach rhythm.

excursions.label: Mnemba Snorkeling & Trip to the North

paje.title: Paje
paje.text: A breezy east-coast base for kitesurfing, lagoon walks, casual beach stays, and active ocean days.

arusha.title: Arusha
arusha.text: The main launch point for northern Tanzania safaris, Kilimanjaro add-ons, and smooth connections into Tarangire, Manyara, Ngorongoro, and Serengeti.

serengeti.title: Serengeti
serengeti.text: The big-name safari anchor: migration plains, predators, balloon options, and fly-in routes from Zanzibar.

ngorongoro.title: Ngorongoro
ngorongoro.text: High-density wildlife, crater landscapes, black rhino chances, and a strong fit for short Tanzania safari routes.

tarangire.title: Tarangire
tarangire.text: A strong short-safari choice for elephants, baobabs, and easier routing with Ngorongoro.

manyara.title: Lake Manyara
manyara.text: A compact northern-circuit stop for lake views, birdlife, forest, and a gentle wildlife day between Tarangire and Ngorongoro.

lake-natron.title: Lake Natron
lake-natron.text: Remote soda-lake landscapes, flamingo breeding grounds, hot springs, Maasai villages, and volcanic scenery near Ol Doinyo Lengai.

olduvai.title: Olduvai Gorge
olduvai.text: A human-origins stop between Ngorongoro and Serengeti, good for guests who want archaeology and context alongside wildlife.

maasai.title: Maasai Villages
maasai.text: A cultural layer for guests who want village time, Maasai traditions, local cooking, and a more human story around the northern circuit.

kilimanjaro.title: Mount Kilimanjaro
kilimanjaro.text: Tanzania’s mountain icon, available as a day experience or as the anchor for a longer climb, safari, and Zanzibar combination.

materuni.title: Materuni
materuni.text: A softer Kilimanjaro foothills day with waterfall hiking, coffee farms, Chagga culture, and mountain scenery.

mikumi.title: Mikumi National Park
mikumi.text: One of the fastest mainland safari choices from Zanzibar, with lions, giraffes, buffalo, zebra, and simpler logistics for short stays.

selous.title: Nyerere National Park
selous.text: A wilder southern choice for boat safaris, walking safaris, fewer vehicles, and quick fly-in options from Zanzibar.

saadani.title: Saadani National Park
saadani.text: A rare Indian Ocean safari setting where beach, river, and bush meet, best for guests who want a different coastal wilderness feel.

udzungwa.title: Udzungwa Mountains
udzungwa.text: Rainforest hikes, waterfalls, rare primates, and a nature-heavy mountain counterpoint to Mikumi or southern safari routes.

ruaha.title: Ruaha National Park
ruaha.text: A remote southern park known for big-cat country, baobab valleys, fewer vehicles, and a stronger wilderness feel than the classic north.

mahale.title: Mahale Mountains & Lake Tanganyika
mahale.text: A premium western circuit for wild chimpanzee trekking, remote lakefront lodges, forest walks, and Lake Tanganyika downtime.

katavi.title: Katavi National Park
katavi.text: A far-west wilderness for serious safari travellers, dry-season floodplain herds, lion coalitions, hippo pools, and very few vehicles.
